title: Datadog's stock jumps 31% on crushing earnings beat, showing there's still hope for software
text: Datadog's Strong Earnings Drive 31 Percent Stock Surge. Financial Performance: The company reported adjusted earnings of 60 cents per share beating the consensus estimate of 51 cents. Revenue reached 1 billion a 32 percent year-over-year increase that significantly outpaced analyst expectations of 931.8 million. Net income more than doubled to 52.6 million from 24.6 million in the prior year. Updated Guidance: Management raised full-year earnings guidance to 2.36-2.44 per share from the previous 2.08-2.16 range. The company also increased revenue guidance to 4.3-4.34 billion up from 4.06-4.1 billion. Market Impact: The stock gained over 30 percent during trading bringing year-to-date gains to approximately 38 percent. The results also lifted other software companies including Snowflake and MongoDB. AI Momentum: Chief Executive Oliver Pomel noted that AI as a growth driver alongside cloud migration represents transformative new technology. The company recently signed seven-figure and eight-figure deals with AI research divisions of major technology firms.
